FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TimePicker

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI TimePicker: a single Button FACE split into Hour | Minute | AM/PM text columns (equal star widths, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:234-239) separated by 1px vertical spacers, and on tap opens ONE TimePickerFlyout presenter via the Overlay service (the whole face is a single FlyoutButton, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:232). The popup is placed so the centered 40px accent highlight band overlays the face (DateTimePickerFlyoutHelper.cpp:39-71) and is width-matched to the face's realized width (TimePickerFlyout_Partial.cpp:196-200). It hosts side-by-side LOOPING selector columns (item height 40) — the shared FluentGpu.Controls.DateTimeLoopColumn — under the highlight band, with a 41px Accept(E8FB) / Dismiss(E711) bar. SelectedTime is nullable (null shows the hour/minute/AM placeholders, the WinUI HasNoTime state); ClockIdentifier selects the 12/24-hour clock and MinuteIncrement coarsens the minute column.

public sealed class TimePicker : Component

Fields

ClockIdentifier

"12HourClock" (default, TimePicker IDL) or "24HourClock" — the 24h clock drops the AM/PM column.

public string ClockIdentifier

Field Value

string

Header

WinUI Header — shown above the face (HeaderContentPresenter, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:231).

public string? Header

Field Value

string

IsEnabled 

public bool IsEnabled

Field Value

bool

MinuteIncrement

WinUI MinuteIncrement (0-59; 0 behaves as 1): the minute column lists 0, inc, 2·inc, …

public int MinuteIncrement

Field Value

int

OnTimeChanged 

public Action<TimeOnly?>? OnTimeChanged

Field Value

Action<TimeOnly?>

SelectedTime

Caller-owned selected time; null shows the placeholders (HasNoTime, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:220-225). A fallback signal is used when null.

public Signal<TimeOnly?>? SelectedTime

Field Value

Signal<TimeOnly?>

WinUI ClockIdentifier values (TimePicker_Partial.cpp:45 s_strTwelveHourClock).

Create()

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
